reviews.review_title: Great way to simply play chess in VR!
reviews.review_description:
I'm happy to see the continued development of this app. The integration of Lichess is a great addition for those who want to compete with that established player base in ranked or unranked games, though not all time limits are available. There are some custom limits such as 6+3 and 20+10, along with regular 10+0, 10+5 and 30+0.

The mixed reality features are more than simply showing your surroundings. You can position the board and adjust its size and height. Placing the UI buttons on the table was a smart design choice that gives you physical resistance against your finger which builds immersion.

The experimental features of incorporating physical board and pieces is really cool, but is still limited by the quality of this headset's passthrough video, and it's not easy lining everything up perfectly; so when you grip a physical piece the app doesn't always register the hand tracking as picking up the digital piece (which is hidden but still overlaying the board). It's still fun to play around with and I hope to see it continue to progress, but my preferred way of playing is with the digital board and pieces, which are simple but attractive with a classic design and wood grain texture.

For me, this was a perfect substitute for owning a very expensive electronic/smart chessboard, and a great use of mixed reality.

First, let me say I love hand tracking and mixed reality, I think it's a great addition to VR. I love that the devs are using hand tracking and mixed reality; it's the reason I bought the game. With all that said, I think the game should also have an option to use the controllers. Hand tracking is a great feature, but at this level, it could be a tedious chore getting the board just right when you just want to jump on and play a quick game or two. 

I love how this game isn't bloated with different chess variants, wacky music, in the app store, leader boards.... it's just a simple flat board with pieces and basic chess setup options, I love it! 🥰

This game requires an internet connection for solo play, I don't like that. This game really needs an offline mode, in case we're traveling and just want to play versus the AI or a player in the same room as us.
Unhappy that I played it for 10 to 15 mins and didn't get a refund when requested !!

reviews.review_title: Its a good base but room for improvement
reviews.review_description:
I think the base is good and the MR feature is actually pretty awesome. 

- Calibration of the chessboard is complicated because Handtracking sometimes doesnt work accurate

- Online is empty. Maybe look into some sort of Lichess Integration, that would be pretty sick.

- Rematch Button? Should be a thing

- With a real chessboard sometimes my moves get stuck. Maybe integrate a "Reset Move" Button?

- Maybe some skins so the board is a bit nicer to look at

This application really goes new ways. I love how I am able to use my physical chess set to play. 
Definitely a glimpse of the future!
Multiplayer with my friend works flawlessly and really feels like he is sitting across the board.

I love the UI/UX interface of this app. Unfortunately i have issues with the AI where it will not make any moves whenever i start the game. I also have not been able to get into a game with anyone. I don’t want to refund this app because i can see its growth potential but it’s unusable atm. Are there any updates that will fix this issue coming soon?


Edit: The AI has been completely fixed and I see that developer is working real hard to make mixed reality chess a reality. The only thing I’m looking forward to is a better matchmaking system.
I like the mixed reality concept in this chess game, but the hand tracking is really bad compared to some free trials chess games in the lab.

This is the first social mixed reality game I ever played in Multiplayer. It it done very well! With the other player as avatar next to the chessboard and the mixed environment of my living room, I had the feeling of the other person really being in the same room as myself. Moving the chess pieces myself with my fingers through handtracking also gave it an extra boost of playing a real game vs. clicking items with my controller. The voice quality during the session was very good. I also played one match offline against an AI which reacted very good as well. It may be good to configure the expert level on the AI in a future update. Everyone interested in chess should clearly check out this innovative app!

Gameplay is very very good i have 2 complaints i wish when u accidentally move a piece to the wrong place and u wanna move it back but ur physical piece is in its same location i want a button to reverse the action and put it back in the last place
and my second complaint isnt the devs fault theres just not any players online to play with but thats not the devs fault i think the most the devs could do to fix this problem is some sponsors or something but i love it and its a great game no hate this is just critisizm

reviews.review_title: Love the game but how do you invite?
reviews.review_description: Really love this game and so far it works great but so far me and my friend haven't been able to work out how to invite each other to our game? There must be a way surely

developer_response.body:
Thank you! Currently you can simply open a game and wait for your friend. Your friend should then be able to see the game directly on his chess-board.
The direct invitation functionality is on our roadmap.
If you have any questions just contact me via contact@mr-chess.com I am happy to help!

reviews.review_description: This is a very polished game and allows you to play on a real or virtual board but does not have quite enough people to find matches quickly.

developer_response.body:
Hi firewry, of course I am trying to onboard new users, in order to fill the lobby, but until then I recommend joining our Discord to setup play sessions. Check out www.mr-chess.com to join the Discord.
Currently I am looking into the integration of chess services but I can't give you a timeline yet.

reviews.review_description: I have played against ai and my first impressions are very good. It really does feel like you are playing chess with a person in front of you.

developer_response.body:
Hi Tyler905 that is exactly what I wanted to achieve with this application. Thank you for your feedback! Feel free to join our discord to connect with other players: 
www.mr-chess.com

reviews.review_description:
Update: Thanks for the feedback and update. Now the App and is running well. Good and impressive experience with these Chess App at the Q3. 
This App is not running and stops already after the initial screen where you can select the mode. I am frustrated that I have spend money on it.

reviews.review_title: Already works well, lots of potential
reviews.review_description:
I saw this on LinkedIn and purchased it to play vs AI and with friends. Both the virtual board and experimental MR mode work surprisingly well, with occasional difficulties recognizing my fingers picking up or dropping pieces.

Right now, there is no way to invite a specific friend, unfortunately. But if you both launch a game at the same time, the likelihood is very high you will be paired up. That’s one important feature I hope is coming soon.

Best of luck to the dev, this will be great! Five stars because it’s still experimental and worth supporting.

This mixed reality paired with my chess board  let's me practice over the board like never before, allowing me to play with virtual pieces against a bot in MR, Incredible! 

4 stars only because it can be improved as it's a bit clunky - for instance choosing your chessboard size. You have to move your hand to all these weird angles and it's hard to setup for a chess board. You have to move your fingers to corners and it registers half the time, board set up needs to be smoother. Maybe let's us touch the 4 corners of a chess board to choose the size, or aim and pinch. 


I have a bug where the white pieces are always invisible and I have to turn off the pieces and turn them back on to fix it. 

Lichess or Chess com implementation would be cool.

game pgn exporting so we can review games after we play right now you can't review games you have to exit then reset to play again and it's a bit annoying.   

Game engine analysis would be great as well to study.

Overall a must for Mixed reality enthusiasts and chess lovers.

It's pretty good but it I didn't see a way to show a moves list on the table during game. This would be very helpful to ensure that the virtual board records the move I intended. In a game, apparently it thought one of my knights was on a different square and I don't know how it happened. Needs a move list so you can confirm it recorded the move you intended.

reviews.review_title: Looking forward to playing the game...
reviews.review_description:
I got the game a few days ago and I was not able to start it because I was holding the controller...🫤 The developers were kind enough to explain this to me...
It now works and I am now looking forward to my first game against a real person.
PS to developers
If this happened to me it is very possible it will happen to others... what about a warning card on the first window saying something like ditch the controller 😁

developer_response.body:
Hi Eric, thanks for updating your rating and your feedback. I will make sure to include it in the next update.
Original reply: 
Hello Eric, please make sure that handtracking is activated. You need to push the button with one of your index fingers by physically moving your hand towards the button. Your index finger needs to pierce through the button. 
The application uses hand-tracking (you can't use it with controllers) and direct touch, which means you need to "physically" push the button. 
If the problem persists I am happy to provide a short tutorial video for you. 
Please contact me under contact@mr-chess.com

reviews.review_title: Good idea, but needs more work
reviews.review_description:
The idea to mix virtual and material pieces is great, in practice I found the concept working not smoothly and reliable, I did not get deeper than a few moves before the software blocked. All virtual worked better, I could end matches. 
The biggest weakness however is, as is so often the case with chess games on consoles, that the AI engine is really too weak. I do not expect this to play like Stockfish or Hiarcs or Fritz or something like these, but what the computer here plays is so bad that it is demotivating to even care any longer. There are hundreds of privatel developed engines available. Just knowing the rules of how to legally move pieces in chess does not enable a coder to code a usable chess engine. I recommend to reach out to the authors of said public engines whether they might not be interested in being included in the project, with their engines.
Three stars because I am in a good mood and wish this project well, but 2 would be more realistic. Add a better engine and fix mixed virtual-material mode to work reliable: then it would become 4-5 stars, it would depend.

developer_response.body:
Hi Skybird,
thanks for your rating!
Playing with physical pieces is currently experimental. Nevertheless the game should not block at any point. Please use the menu at the bottom of you chess board and push the button with the "rook" and the "eye" symbol. This displays all virtual pieces and you can see what went wrong.
Regarding the AI difficulty: You can choose different difficulties (from 1 to 8, where 8 is the hardest). I am pretty sure the AI plays well enough.
Let me know if that helped and take care!

reviews.review_title: Great app to play with friends
reviews.review_description:
For me, this is the first app that captures the essence of mixed reality elegantly.
The core functionality works robustly, the chessboard calibration process takes a little practice, but works as well. 
All in all a very innovative project that I happily support!
